title: MEA board sacks two managers, targets general manager

title: Begich topples Stevens in Senate race
summary: Alaska Sen. Ted Stevens lost his job to Mark Begich Tuesday, putting an end to the era of Uncle Ted as the dominant force in Alaska politics. Begich widened his lead to 3,724 votes in Tuesday's count of absentee and questioned ballots. The lead is insurmountable, as the only votes left to count are approximately 2,500 ballots from overseas.

title: Anchorage man gunned down in Spenard
summary: Alaska News Anchorage man gunned down in Spenard Author: Patty Sullivan, Anchorage Daily News Updated: 1 day ago Published November 22, 1997 Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share via Email Share on Tumblr Share on Reddit Share on LinkedIn Share on Google Plus Print article A man at a Spenard house was fatally shot Friday morning after he walked next door to complain about someone shooting a pellet gun at the residence, Anchorage police said. Delbert White, 32, of Anchorage was shot in the back of the head and the hand with an assault rifle, police spokesman Ron McGee said. He was taken to Providence Alaska Medical Center where he died shortly before noon, McGee said. The man next door, 32-year-old Bret F. Maness, turned himself in to police. He was charged with first-degree murder and w…

title: Walker wants to divert cash for ANWR oil study to new 911 system
summary: A new proposal from Alaska Gov. Bill Walker would set aside $9.5 million for a new statewide 911 system – mostly cash that he previously earmarked for studying the oil and gas potential of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.

title: Walker wants to divert cash for ANWR oil study to new 911 system
summary: A new proposal from Alaska Gov. Bill Walker would set aside $9.5 million for a new statewide 911 system – mostly cash that he previously earmarked for studying the oil and gas potential of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.

title: Fresh shock waves hit U.S. markets after Trump tweets, China steps up trade war
summary: All three major stock indexes are negative for the year. The price of gold and of silver, both considered flights to safety, were up on Monday.

title: Community patrols booming in Anchorage amid crime spike
summary: Four patrols have started within the past six months alone -- twice the traditional number of patrols, and mostly in South Anchorage.

title: Florida bank now owns former legislative office building in downtown Anchorage
summary: EverBank settled a lawsuit with an Anchorage development company and says it is planning to put the property on the market.

title: Man found shot to death in car ID’d by Anchorage police
summary: Detectives believe the incident was a targeted, drug-related crime, police said.

title: Trifecta of falling fish revenue is heading for Alaska fishermen, coastal communities
summary: Coming on the heels of an 80 percent crash of cod stocks in the Gulf of Alaska and a 10 percent decline in halibut catches, state fishery managers project a salmon harvest down 34 percent.

title: Big Island shark attack leaves standup paddler in critical condition
summary: A 25-year-old standup paddler was bumped off his board and critically injured by a shark Saturday off the Kona Coast in Hawaii's first shark attack of the year.

title: After getting burned by a previous manager, how can I show employees they can trust me?
summary: Workplace advice: A straightforward answer, with specifics, respects the questioner and initiates trust. Then you prove yourself.

title: Big Island shark attack leaves standup paddler in critical condition
summary: A 25-year-old standup paddler was bumped off his board and critically injured by a shark Saturday off the Kona Coast in Hawaii's first shark attack of the year.

title: Bogoslof volcano makes audio history
summary: Observers have described hearing volcanic thunder in the past, but scientists have been unable to disentangle from the cacophony of bellows and blasts that accompany an explosive eruption.

title: Shulkin says he did not leave office willingly, setting up fight over his successor
summary: Whether Shulkin resigned or was fired would have bearing on who leads the Department of Veterans Affairs until the president's nominee, Ronny L. Jackson, is confirmed by the Senate.

title: Trifecta of falling fish revenue is heading for Alaska fishermen, coastal communities
summary: Coming on the heels of an 80 percent crash of cod stocks in the Gulf of Alaska and a 10 percent decline in halibut catches, state fishery managers project a salmon harvest down 34 percent.

title: Alaska gas line corporation hires contractors with D.C. connections
summary: The Alaska Gasline Development Corp. has signed contracts with former White House communications director Mike Dubke and a former aide to Sen. Lisa Murkowski, Kevin Sweeney.

title: No inquiry into Gaza border deaths, says Israeli defense minister
summary: The March of Return to mark the anniversary of land appropriation by Israel in 1976 drew tens of thousands of Gaza residents to points along the border fence on Friday.

title: FBI surveillance audio: Vic Kohring trial
summary: FBI surveillance audio: Vic Kohring trial

title: 'No more DACA deal,' Trump says as he threatens to 'stop' NAFTA if Mexico doesn't better secure border
summary: Firing off tweets, the president directed congressional Republicans to pass tough new anti-immigration legislation.

title: Republican Senate appointee whose Facebook posts drew attention withdraws from consideration
summary: The Alaska Republican Party said it was replacing its suggestion of Tom Braund for the vacant seat with a Palmer woman, Vicki Wallner.

title: ‘Enough is enough!’ Students demand action on gun violence in nation’s capital
summary: Huge crowds gathered in Washington and other cities to protest the mass shooting in Parkland, Florida, and the everyday gunfire that scars kids' lives.

title: 3 killed in France in shooting and supermarket hostage-taking
summary: Three people were killed in southwestern France on Friday when a gunman held up a car, opened fire on police and then took hostages in a supermarket, screaming Allahu Akbar.

title: When freedom feels like extortion: Bail bondsmen accused of exploiting poor clients
summary: Commercial bail has grown into a $2 billion industry, and bond agents have become the payday lenders of the criminal justice world. But bondsmen have extraordinary powers that most lenders do not.

title: Russia will expel U.S. diplomats and close a consulate in St. Petersburg
summary: Russia said Thursday it will respond in kind to the expulsion of diplomats by Western countries in a row over the poisoning of an ex-spy in Britain.

title: When cyclists collide with bears
summary: Maulings are rare, and the chances of being injured or killed by a bear while riding a bike are slim. But biking increases your odds of injury in the unlikely event you encounter a bear at close range.

title: Iconic in Alaska, classic Xtratuf boot gets a redesign
summary: The Xtratuf company says the Legacy 2.0 has better slip resistance and a bigger shaft to accommodate larger American calves. But the original will still be available.

title: 5 people charged in meth-related robbery and assault in Sitka
summary: The crime involved drugs, guns and squatting on a yacht in the scenic Southeast Alaska community of roughly 9,000 people, police say.

title: Here’s a look at the airlines that carry rural Alaskans that final mile, whether in Ketchikan or Kaktovik
summary: Scott McMurren: There are dozens of smaller airlines around the state that move travelers between long, paved runways and a gravel strip on the edge of the wilderness.
